title: Commanded quadruped mobility from simulation to governed hardware
proposition: Develop a compact four-legged robot that follows planar velocity and yaw commands, survives bounded disturbances, and carries reproducible evidence from MuJoCo through shadow hardware commissioning.

morphology.notes: Free-base quadruped with three actuated joints per leg in the current development Assembly.

capabilityStages.name: Command-conditioned locomotion
capabilityStages.question: Can the robot execute stop, forward, reverse, lateral, yaw, delay, and disturbance commands without gate violations?
exitCriteria: All gating cases in the locked command-tracking Benchmark pass.
Every accepted Controller remains compatible with the declared Assembly interface.

capabilityStages.name: Transitions, braking, and visible motion quality
capabilityStages.question: Can the robot redirect and stop cleanly without trading away yaw settling or foot-contact quality?
exitCriteria: All gating transition cases pass without regression against command-tracking.
A human-reviewed witness shows accepted motion without unresolved severe visual hypotheses.

capabilityStages.name: Robustness and sim-to-real evidence
capabilityStages.question: Does the selected robot remain feasible across held-out plant variation and governed shadow commissioning?
exitCriteria: All gating held-out plant cases pass with immutable Policy and calibration provenance.
Shadow hardware capture preserves safety, state ABI, and Judge identities.

nonGoals: Treating aggregate reward as a substitute for capability gates
Granting hardware actuation authority from simulation evidence alone
